title: Rebuild status
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild status? Post by Podferio » Sat Mar 19, 2011 10:14 am Ok guys so I heared other animes got delayed due to what happened to Japan. I haven't been here lately so what about evangelion? Is Gainax ok there? I mean is the project still doing fine or also got damaged?

[]: Re: Rebuild status? Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Mar 19, 2011 10:20 am Podferio wrote: Ok guys so I heared other animes got delayed due to what happened to Japan. I haven't been here lately so what about evangelion? Is Gainax ok there? I mean is the project still doing fine or also got damaged?

[]: Post by Warren Peace » Sat Mar 19, 2011 10:25 am "Development Hell" is overstating things. That implies a production in turnaround, which is a leap (there's too much money in this for them not to finish). It'll get done. Just because we know zilch about its progress doesn't mean there isn't any. They could release the trailer next week for all we know.

[]: Post by Hatsumi92 » Sat Mar 19, 2011 11:00 am Wasn't it reported that Q was going to be "Full budget"? They also make it sound like 2.0 was being made while 1.0 was being made but 3.0 wasn't being made (script aside and the preview in 2.0 if you count that as actual footage) until 2.0 was in the cinemas for a little while. Movies take awhile and if you want something of actual quality it takes a rather long time. Khara isn't exactly a huge company too. The name and it's status is huge but there's not many people in it. Also Sadamoto starting up NGE's manga again may be hinting at some kind of announcement. Oh and the Earthquake and Tsunami will not affect anything within Rebuild (as in content) at all.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 19, 2011 12:05 pm They have been hiring animators and established a twitter page. They are obviously working on something - and as said twitter page recently contained a message expressing condolences for the people in the north, there was obviously someone there to write it.

[]: Post by Hatsumi92 » Sat Mar 19, 2011 12:26 pm Kendrix wrote: They have been hiring animators and established a twitter page. They are obviously working on something - and as said twitter page recently contained a message expressing condolences for the people in the north, there was obviously someone there to write it.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 19, 2011 12:32 pm Hatsumi92 wrote: Anno written that. Not sure if he, himself posted it, but he written it.

[]: Post by CORE » Sat Mar 19, 2011 2:15 pm you don't start a twitter page this late into the twitter fad with out a reason. They held off the craze and now have some thing to say. Expect Q soon.

[]: Post by Ornette » Sat Mar 19, 2011 2:24 pm CORE wrote: you don't start a twitter page this late into the twitter fad with out a reason. They held off the craze and now have some thing to say. Expect Q soon.

[]: Post by TaintedHero » Sat Mar 19, 2011 6:19 pm Ornette wrote: 株式会社カラー公式アカウントを開設しました。『ヱヴァンゲリヲン新劇場版』をはじめ、スタジオカラー作品などの最新情報をお知らせしていきます。どうぞよろしくお願いいたします

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sun Mar 20, 2011 9:40 am Ornette wrote: Especially when their first tweet was: Essentially an outlet for making the latest announcements including "Evangelion".

[]: Post by Xard » Sun Mar 20, 2011 9:46 am Given that Khara got credited for some CG work in new Macross film (and it premiered as recently as 26th Feb) it's obvious they still have the full staff employed and working. and it's not hard to see what it is they're working on. Add together with this registering to Twitter and I say we get some major news before summer. I expect October/December release date which will be postponed to January-February next year, knowing Anno

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sun Mar 20, 2011 9:53 am Xard wrote: which will be postponed to January-February next year, knowing Anno

[]: Post by Safeas112 » Sun Mar 20, 2011 11:02 pm Xard wrote: Given that Khara got credited for some CG work in new Macross film (and it premiered as recently as 26th Feb) it's obvious they still have the full staff employed and working. and it's not hard to see what it is they're working on. Add together with this registering to Twitter and I say we get some major news before summer. I expect October/December release date which will be postponed to January-February next year, knowing Anno

[]: Post by Sachi » Sun Mar 20, 2011 11:06 pm Safeas112 wrote: And most likely a March release for english subtitles so we can understand what's going on with the characters ? (or am I pushing it there with a March date?).

[]: Post by TaintedHero » Sun Mar 20, 2011 11:13 pm Safeas112 wrote: And most likely a March release for english subtitles so we can understand what's going on with the characters ? (or am I pushing it there with a March date?).

[]: Post by Safeas112 » Sun Mar 20, 2011 11:20 pm TaintedHero wrote: Nonsense, you have some 9 months before a speculated release to learn Japanese. I can't stand cam rips, I will wait however long it takes for at least 720p and some decent subs.

[]: Post by Warren Peace » Mon Mar 21, 2011 12:02 am Actually, 2.0's cam-rip wasn't that bad. After the first five minutes, anyway. A lot of people checked out when they saw how dark the Mari sequence looked, but the rest of the film was (relatively) passable.

[]: Post by Safeas112 » Mon Mar 21, 2011 12:14 am Warren Peace wrote: Actually, 2.0's cam-rip wasn't that bad. After the first five minutes, anyway. A lot of people checked out when they saw how dark the Mari sequence looked, but the rest of the film was (relatively) passable.

[]: Post by Warren Peace » Mon Mar 21, 2011 12:48 am Oh yeah, of course you want DVD/BD quality when that's an option. But if we get another full-length cam in that quality, we'll be lucky.
